content.problemStatement:
Chef recently accepted a teaching fellowship at a prestigious culinary institution. The fellowship does not begin for a while yet, so Chef wants to use the remaining free time for a nice vacation. There are NNN cities (numbered 111 through NNN) connected by MMM roads. For each valid iii, there are LiL_iLi‚Äč tourist attractions in city iii. Chef is initially in city 111 and he will teach in city NNN. On each day of his vacation, Chef wants to perform one of the following actions:


Travel to a city with a higher number than his current city which is directly connected to his current city by a road. At the end of his vacation, Chef must be in city NNN.
Visit a tourist attraction in his current city. Chef may visit each tourist attraction repeatedly (on different days).
Chef has not yet decided for how many days the vacation should last. He has QQQ queries described by a sequence D1,D2,‚Ä¶,DQD_1, D_2, \ldots, D_QD1‚Äč,D2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,DQ‚Äč. For each query, i.e. for each iii (1‚Č§i‚Č§Q1 \le i \le Q1‚Č§i‚Č§Q), he would like to know the number of different vacation plans if he wants his vacation to last exactly DiD_iDi‚Äč days. Since these numbers may be large, compute them modulo 1,000,000,0071,000,000,0071,000,000,007.

Two vacation plans (for a fixed duration of the vacation) are considered different if there is at least one day such that Chef performs different actions in the two plans on that day. Visiting different tourist attractions also counts as different actions.
constraints: Subtasks**Subtask #1 (5 points):** Di‚Č§2,000D_i \le 2,000Di‚Äč‚Č§2,000 for each valid iii
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains three space-separated integers NNN, MMM and QQQ.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers L1,L2,‚Ä¶,LNL_1, L_2, \ldots, L_NL1‚Äč,L2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,LN‚Äč.
Each of the next MMM lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv denoting that cities uuu and vvv are connected by a road.
The last line contains QQQ space-separated integers D1,D2,‚Ä¶,DQD_1, D_2, \ldots, D_QD1‚Äč,D2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,DQ‚Äč.
content.outputFormat: For each query, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äē the number of possible vacations modulo 1,000,000,0071,000,000,0071,000,000,007.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Since there is no road directly connecting city 111 to city 444, there are no valid vacations that only last 1 day, so the answer to the first query is 000.
There are two ways to get to city 444 in 2 days ? either travel from city 111 to city 222 on the first day and from city 222 to city 444 on the second day, or travel from city 111 to city 333 on the first day and from city 333 to city 444 on the second day. Neither option gives Chef any time for sightseeing, though.
With 3 days, Chef can spend one day at a tourist attraction and the other two days travelling, with 30 possible trips in total.
